summary: All code_refs and the modal-v2v source figures verify against ground truth. SmartTurn append_audio buffers int16 PCM with monotonic timestamps; silence net ends turn at _silence_ms >= stop_secs*1000 with STOP_SECS=3; pre_speech 500ms; max 8s keeping the end; v3 resamples to 16kHz, pads/truncates to 8s, computes Whisper log-mel, thresholds sigmoid at >0.5; audio-only; v2 is pipecat-ai/smart-turn-v2. Modal blog confirms Silero VAD + SmartTurn pairing, CPU-only, yields to interruptions but not mid-sentence pauses.

claims.claim: append_audio buffers raw int16 PCM with monotonic timestamps, sets _speech_triggered on speech
claims.found: def append_audio(...): audio_int16 = np.frombuffer(buffer, dtype=np.int16); self._audio_buffer.append((time.monotonic(), audio_int16)) ... self._speech_triggered = True
if self._silence_ms >= self._stop_ms: ... state = EndOfTurnState.COMPLETE (lines 132-136); _stop_ms = stop_secs*1000 at :70

claims.claim: caps to max_duration_secs=8 keeping the end
claims.found: max_samples = int(self._params.max_duration_secs * self.sample_rate); if len(segment_audio) > max_samples: segment_audio = segment_audio[-max_samples:]  (MAX_DURATION_SECONDS=8 at :29)
def _resample_to_model_rate at :123; soxr.resample(audio_array, actual_rate, _MODEL_SAMPLE_RATE=16000, quality='HQ') at :136; called at :158
# Truncate to 8 seconds (keeping the end) or pad to 8 seconds; audio_array = truncate_audio_to_last_n_seconds(audio_array, n_seconds=8) at :161; pads with zeros at beginning

claims.claim: thresholds sigmoid: prediction = 1 if probability > 0.5 else 0
model sees audio only, no transcript text
claims.found: _predict_endpoint takes only audio_array; ONNX input is 'input_features' (log-mel of audio); no text input anywhere
smart_turn_model_path = 'pipecat-ai/smart-turn-v2' at :72; uses torch / Wav2Vec2 model (deprecated in favor of V3)

claims.claim: Modal pairs Silero VAD + SmartTurn so bots yield to interruptions but don't respond prematurely during brief mid-sentence pauses
claims.found: 'Pipecat integrates Silero VAD ... + their own turn model SmartTurn ... bots yield to interruptions but don't respond prematurely during brief mid-sentence pauses.'

claims.claim: CPU-cheap enough that Modal runs whole bot on CPU
claims.found: 'Even with Silero VAD + SmartTurn, Pipecat bots only need CPUs -> CPU-only container for the long-running stateful bot'

claims.claim: Modal found VAD+turn-gated batch STT beats streaming STT on total v2v latency
claims.found: 'using Pipecat's local VAD + turn detection to segment audio and passing that to Parakeet was FASTER than the open-weights streaming STT implementations' / 'the only thing that matters for total voice-to-voice latency is the final transcript time'
